audit_items.proof_trace: All sk_buff allocations in tls.c follow a documented ownership-transfer model: `__nfp_ccm_mbox_communicate` and `nfp_ccm_mbox_post` always free the skb on error via their `err_free_skb` label (ccm_mbox.c:592-593, 684-685), while on success they return without freeing — callers then explicitly free via `dev_consume_skb_any`. In `nfp_net_tls_add`, `skb_get` intentionally holds a second reference across the communicate call to allow key-wiping and reply inspection; both references are released on every success and error path without leaks.
